posts.content:
CHI 2026 is right around the corner. Here is some cool stuff from my lab.

1. Counterfactual Explanations for Agentic Workflows (github.com/eilab-gt/eil...) . A new API for making AI pipelines that automatically makes your pipeline explainable. Counterfactuals let you explore what *could* happen.
1/
posts.highlights: The post introduces a new API for creating explainable AI pipelines through counterfactual explanations, enabling users to explore alternative outcomes in agentic workflows. This approach enhances transparency by allowing investigation of what could happen under different conditions.
posts.worthReading: It offers practical insights into making complex AI systems more interpretable, which is crucial for debugging and trust in automated decision-making processes.

posts.highlights: The post presents a minimalistic, philosophical question about identity and self-awareness, potentially reflecting on AI's evolving role in society. Its brevity invites interpretation, suggesting deeper contemplation about human-AI relationships.
posts.worthReading: It sparks thought on existential themes in tech discourse, encouraging readers to reflect on AI's impact on personal and collective identity.

posts.content: a bit over 7 days out from the Gemma 4 release and it's models are outpacing (slightly) the equivalent Qwen 3.5 models on downloads. Big numbers!
posts.highlights: The Gemma 4 model release is showing early traction by slightly outpacing equivalent Qwen 3.5 models in download metrics just over a week post-launch. This suggests competitive performance or features that are resonating with users in the crowded open-source AI model space.
posts.worthReading: It provides timely insight into real-world adoption trends for emerging open-source AI models, which can inform decisions about model selection and investment.

posts.content: Regardless of one’s personal beliefs about AI or Mr Altman, this is not appropriate
posts.highlights: The post critiques the tone or approach of discourse around AI leadership, suggesting that personal beliefs should not override professional decorum. It implies that discussions about influential figures like Sam Altman require measured, appropriate engagement regardless of one's stance on AI.
posts.worthReading: It prompts reflection on how we discuss tech leaders and AI ethics, emphasizing the importance of respectful dialogue in polarized debates.

posts.content: Fortunately, I speak fluent LLM, and... no, dear emailer, I do not have bandwidth to mentor a high school student at this time, sorry.
posts.highlights: The post humorously highlights the growing expectation for AI experts to provide free mentorship, reflecting broader societal pressures on tech professionals. It uses irony to comment on the imbalance between public interest in AI and the practical constraints of experts' time.
posts.worthReading: It offers a relatable, witty perspective on the challenges faced by AI researchers in managing public engagement, making it both entertaining and insightful for those in tech communities.

posts.content:
The Amazing Digital Circus Episode 9 "The Last Act" is coming out June 19th!

And get this:... itwill be shown (with episode 8) in THEATRES June 4th!! 🤩🤩🤩

Trailer below. 👇

www.youtube.com/watch?v=AjFk...
posts.highlights: The post announces the theatrical release of 'The Amazing Digital Circus' episodes 8 and 9 on June 4th, followed by the online premiere of episode 9 on June 19th. This represents a significant crossover event where a popular digital series transitions to a traditional cinema experience.
posts.worthReading: It showcases an innovative distribution strategy blending digital content with theatrical releases, highlighting evolving trends in media consumption and creator-audience engagement.

posts.content:
More Bluesky icons spotted in the wild! 🦋😀

Just discovered a game called "The Castle"🏰 by @pointpixeladv.bsky.social : a #retrogames adventure inspired by Maniac Mansion, sale for just a few bucks on Good ol' Games (which I discovered is *also* on Bluesky) @gog.com !

www.gog.com/en/game/the_...
posts.highlights: The post highlights Bluesky's expanding ecosystem through user discovery of both a retro game and its distribution platform (GOG) having Bluesky presence. It showcases how Bluesky is becoming a discovery hub for niche tech/gaming communities.
posts.worthReading: It demonstrates real-world Bluesky adoption beyond social networking, showing how users organically discover interconnected tech/gaming content through the platform.

posts.content:
Real-world telemetry data and Internet of Things collide with scientific machine learning #sciml? Devices which can learn how to adapt to reality and continually improve their efficiency. #UDEs with #Dyad => better thermal efficiency.

See : arxiv.org/abs/2603.15943

#julialang #AI4Science
posts.highlights: The post discusses how real-world telemetry data from IoT devices can be integrated with scientific machine learning (SciML) to create adaptive systems. It specifically mentions using Universal Differential Equations (UDEs) with Dyad to improve thermal efficiency, suggesting a practical application of AI in optimizing physical systems.
posts.worthReading: It connects emerging IoT data streams with advanced machine learning techniques for tangible efficiency gains, and provides a concrete arXiv reference for technical details.
